title: v2ecoli plateau vs Beulig 2025 batch trajectories (preliminary, refined)
caption: log-y biomass(t) — v2ecoli at cpa=1e9 at two durations (60-sim-min reference + 175-sim-min salvaged from a 6h sim killed at the memory wall) overlaid against the 5 best-sampled Beulig reactors' batch-phase trajectories. The 175-min run confirms v2ecoli's biomass plateaus around 4.3 mg/L per division cycle, NOT because duration is short but because single_daughters caps total biomass at cell_mass × cpa for one lineage.
simulations: v2ecoli: aggregator-cpa1e9-multigen (seed=0, 60 sim-min, gen 1-2) + aggregator-cpa1e9-batch-prefix-multigen (seed=0, salvaged 175 sim-min from a 6h run terminated at the 128GB memory ceiling). Beulig: DDB_PD_006_AMBR_R9, DDB_PD_009_AMBR_R10, DDB_PD_009_AMBR_R11, DDB_PD_006_AMBR_R8, DDB_PD_038_AMBR_R10 from reactor_OD_data.csv, batch phase only.
interpretation:
This chart settles the architectural question conclusively. v2ecoli at cpa=1e9 plateaus around 4.3 mg/L over 175 sim-min — the trajectory oscillates between ~1 and ~4 mg/L following the division cycle (cell_mass 1100→2300 fg → halve at division), but does NOT accumulate across generations. The single_daughters convention follows ONE lineage; biomass = cell_mass × cpa is bounded by the cell's mass range. Beulig batch peaks at ~9.6 g/L — 3.3 orders above v2ecoli's plateau. Closing this gap requires either: (a) a population composite that tracks accumulating cell count (counter to single_daughters), (b) impossibly-large cpa (~1e12), or (c) fed-batch + surrogate engine (mbp-06 candidate-future-study). mbp-05's comparison harness will surface this diagnostically rather than try to close it via tuning.

[2026-05-29 CAVEAT — single_daughters configuration]: This chart's plateau finding is empirically valid FOR the single_daughters configuration (one daughter followed at each division — the v2ecoli default). The architectural-cap statement is overstated as a blanket claim: with both daughters tracked (--no-single-daughters), v2ecoli's population doubles every generation and biomass DOES accumulate exponentially. The follow-up run aggregator-cpa1e9-multigen --no-single-daughters tests this directly; see chart 03 (companion). The 3.3-order single_daughters plateau gap may be reduced to ~1.2 orders under both_daughters, though full closure to Beulig batch (~9.6 g/L) still requires fed-batch operations + the architectural decisions in mbp-06.
note_on_partial_run: The 6h cpa=1e9 sim was killed at sim_min=175 due to a memory ceiling (sim grew to ~72 GB RSS and was contending with the system's 128 GB cap). The salvaged 175-min trajectory is more than sufficient for the plateau diagnostic; reaching 6h would not change the architectural story.
configuration_caveat: single_daughters=True (v2ecoli default); both_daughters comparison in chart 03
